title: DECR
description: Decrements the integer value of a key by one. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
Decrements the number stored at `key` by one. If the key does not exist, it is set to `0` before performing the operation. An error is returned if the key contains a value of the wrong type or contains a string that can not be represented as integer. This operation is limited to **64 bit signed integers**. See [`INCR`]() for extra information on increment/decrement operations. ## Examples SET mykey "10" DECR mykey SET mykey "234293482390480948029348230948" DECR mykey ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the value of the key after decrementing it. **RESP3:** [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the value of the key after decrementing it.
intro:
Decrements the number stored at `key` by one.
If the key does not exist, it is set to `0` before performing the operation.
An error is returned if the key contains a value of the wrong type or contains a
string that can not be represented as integer.
This operation is limited to **64 bit signed integers**.

See [`INCR`]() for extra information on increment/decrement operations.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Decrements the number stored at `key` by one.
If the key does not exist, it is set to `0` before performing the operation.
An error is returned if the key contains a value of the wrong type or contains a
string that can not be represented as integer.
This operation is limited to **64 bit signed integers**.

See [`INCR`]() for extra information on increment/decrement operations.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
SET mykey "10"
DECR mykey
SET mykey "234293482390480948029348230948"
DECR mykey

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the value of the key after decrementing it.

**RESP3:**

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the value of the key after decrementing it.
